## Main navigation

## Breadcrumb

# Infovox ivox alternative

By
Prince, 16 October, 2021

Infovox ivox isn't working on Mac anymore, I already tried that. Does anyone know of any alternatives for voices that can be downloaded to Mac as an alternative from Infovox voices and the genetic apple voices?

## Options

## Comments

### Wondering the same thing

By Moopie Curran on Saturday, October 16, 2021 - 17:59 [...] By Moopie Curran on Saturday, October 16, 2021 - 17:59

Right now, on my current macs, the infovox voices work, but if I get a new mac, I'll be up the proverbial creek. So I'm wondering the same thing because I convert a LOT of documents to mp;3's to listen to on any device. And I don't know about you, but there aren't nearly enough male voices available for the mac that will work with text to audio converters. Tom, Alex, and Fred are the only game in town as far as human sounding male voices genetically available from apple are concerned. And Fred isn't human sounding. So any help would be greatly appreciated.

### RE: Infovox ivox alternative

By Jurgen on Saturday, November 13, 2021 - 10:55 [...] By Jurgen on Saturday, November 13, 2021 - 10:55

Hello,  
CereVoices work just fine. Of course it's all about if I like the voices. I installed german Alex and didn't like it because of its sluggishness. But that's up to you.  
  
All the best

### Cerevoice and Catalina and above

By Moopie Curran on Saturday, November 13, 2021 - 21:58

Hi,  
Does this voice engine work with Catalina and above? I purchased two or three of the voices, but couldn't seem to put them on my macbook with catalina.

Incredibly expensive recurring subscription fees to bleed customers dry ad infinitum, without any possibility to purchase the software once and for all. There are functionally superior and better organised/structured apps that can be bought instead of rented, which is why this milkingware has been replaced with a better alternative and is now being uninstalled.

If it doesn't change I'll just uninstall it, as I have no interest in a pay plan and it absolutely looks like they're trying to make it so irritating that we end paying, which I have no intention of doing

results.title: iVox Solutions | Outsource Accelerator
results.content:
## About iVox Solutions: Review and comparison

iVox Solutions is a popular contact center company headquartered in Port St. Lucie, Florida. iVox works to partner with established companies to provide call and contact outsourcing solutions to help them recruit and retain clients.

iVox recognizes that acquiring and retaining customers costs companies a lot of money. Because of this, employees upsell and cross-sell clients to create more revenue. iVox employees excel in customer service relations and providing a high-quality customer experience.

iVox Solutions trains its employees to uphold and promote the client’s image in a positive manner and to ensure that each customer interaction accurately reflects the client’s policies and values.

## iVox Solutions Outsourcing [...] ## iVox Solutions Outsourcing

iVox is a contact center that operates out of its headquarters in Port St. Lucie, Florida. Companies outsource their customer service and call center operations to iVox because of its high-quality employees that provide an excellent customer experience. 

It’s estimated that anywhere from 201-500 employees currently work for iVox Solutions. iVox has its main office in Florida but accepts new clients from all over the country. Some of the most popular clients that iVox has are Dish Network, AT&T, ADT, and Duke Energy.

## iVox Solutions’s BPO services and pricings

iVox Solutions offers a wide variety of BPO services, including: 

 Helping businesses change to onshore locations rather than offshore outsourcing [...] iVox Solutions recognizes that acquiring new customers costs more money than retaining existing ones, so iVox allows you to focus your business’s energy on profit and growth while they handle the customer service relations. 

iVox takes the opportunity to increase revenue while handling customer relations. iVox trains its employees to upsell and cross-sell customers to help generate more profit for the client, which most other contact centers fail to do.

## Key executives

### Rob Newton (CEO)

Rob Newton has been the CEO of iVox Solutions since 2003. His goal with iVox is to find innovative solutions to clients’ needs. He graduated from Indian River State College in 2000 with a Bachelor of Science in Business Administration and Management.

## iVox Solutions reviews and comparisons

results.title: iVox Books | Library Ideas
results.content:
Help Desk

Library Ideas

Select Page

## iVOX delights & engages curious young minds.

Request Quote or Demo

A revolutionary take on read-alongs for children ages 2-10. It is our wildly popular VOX Books experience, integrated and expanded into an interactive adventure using state-of-the-art technology.

Whether used as a motivational tool for young readers or simply to add a bit more magic to the reading experience, iVOX is a learning tool that benefits children of all ages.

Available online or as apps for iOS, Android, or Kindle Fire. Simply go to the website or download the app, select a book, and press start to bring favorite stories to life.

## AMAZING FEATURES

### Follow us on [...] Books downloaded when using the iVOX app are accessible in the app even when the device is offline. When using iVOX in a browser, the user must be online at all times for iVOX to function.

##### What are the differences between using iVOX in a browser vs using the iVOX app?

The main benefits to using iVOX in a browser are that no device is needed and that iVOX titles will be as large as the display.

However, users must be online at all times when using a browser, and the manipulation of the image isn’t quite as intuitive, since it involves using the mouse buttons and the scroll wheel. [...] ##### Why is iVOX not working?

It could be that the patron’s device isn’t compatible with the app. All iOS, Android, and Kindle Fire devices should work with the app, but older models may not have the processing speed capable of supporting the animation.

The user might have entered their library credentials incorrectly.

If the user isn’t connected to the internet, they will not be able to checkout and download titles with the app or use iVOX with a browser at all.
